blockingIssues.description: Multi-turn `call_model_input_filter` rewrites are only snapshotted on the first turn, so later filtered continuations are lost from normalized replay/durable state and parity with upstream filtered-history behavior is incomplete.
The feature does not add executable structured-output `max_turns` coverage required by VAL-CORE-009; only plain/streamed tool-loop regressions were added.

Structured-output schemas remain helper-only because the runner/provider request path still hardcodes no output schema, leaving `Agent`/`ModelRequest`/OpenAI payload builders without a structured-output channel.
`OpenAIProvider::with_api(...)` is still overridden by the shared `use_responses_by_default` setting, so explicit per-provider API selection cannot force Chat Completions vs Responses.
Explicit HTTP transport override is still impossible once the shared websocket default is enabled because the per-provider websocket flag is modeled as a plain bool instead of a tri-state override.
Server-conversation delta tracking still loses source attribution after `call_model_input_filter` rewrites an item, so already-sent input can remain in the unsent pool and be replayed on later turns.
`MCPTransportClientFactory` is only an observational side-effect callback and cannot construct or modify the actual transport client/request pipeline, so client-factory parity is incomplete.

Generated streamable-HTTP session ids are derived from the server name instead of true per-session transport state, which does not preserve upstream session-id semantics across fresh/replaced sessions.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` with factual notes that server-managed conversation tracking must preserve source attribution across `call_model_input_filter` rewrites and that structured-output parity requires schema data to flow through the runtime request path.

Updated `.factory/library/parity.md` with MCP transport/session parity notes covering header-vs-option session semantics and the requirement that client-factory parity must affect the real transport client instead of being side-effect-only.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that runtime parity features can legitimately be evidence-only closures for already-correct behavior and can also be narrowly scoped no-behavior hygiene fixes when the feature description explicitly calls for that; document how to handle those cases without forcing fake failing-test-first or upstream-reference steps.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The structured-output/provider review recorded skill-feedback asking for guidance on coverage-only parity closures, and the provider-warning cleanup review showed a correctly scoped warning-only task that did not benefit from the default upstream-read/failing-test-first procedure.

rejectedObservations.observation: Treat the warning-cleanup skill observation as a separate guidance change from the broader runtime-parity-worker clarification.
